_comment: Test-health SLOs for the factory. The history renderers read this (zero-dep JSON) and flag ✅/⚠️ breaches in the ci-history and e2e-history READMEs; the QA Manager reads breaches as its prioritised worklist and tracks the factory converging toward these. Edit to raise/lower the bar — never lower a floor to hide a regression. Floors are grounded in current actuals (2026-06): unit lines ~88%, branches ~74%; e2e screens ~85%, journeys 7/7.

quality._comment: Static-analysis ceilings (counts). These are the eventual-state floors and may be breached today (e.g. tsc has a ~193-error latent backlog because vite build never type-checked) — a breach is the signal that drives cleanup tickets via the QA Manager scorecard. Promote the matching CI check from report-only to GATING only once its count is at 0 and stable.
